title: Tanker report - Week 15
summary:
Clean


LR2
LR2s in the MEG this week have been balanced with a tip towards a softer dynamic. TC1 has shed five points to WS159.38 (a round trip TCE of $37,317 /day) and TC20 has had a $257,000 chunk taken out of it, the index currently sits at $3,971,429 with reports of $3,900,000 currently on subjects. West of Suez, Mediterranean/East LR2s have been relatively sedate this week.

children.title: LR2
children.summary: LR2s in the MEG this week have been balanced with a tip towards a softer dynamic. TC1 has shed five points to WS159.38 (a round trip TCE of $37,317 /day) and TC20 has had a $257,000 chunk taken out of it, the index currently sits at $3,971,429 with reports of $3,900,000 currently on subjects.

children.title: LR1
children.summary: In the MEG, LR1 activity has been reportedly low this week. TC5 has shed 7.85 points to WS199.29 and a round trip TCE of $37,488/day.

children.title: MR
children.summary: MEG MRs have been somewhat fragmented this week with a large influx of enquiry mid-week, including replacements, generating a directionless market. Subsequently the TC17 index has held around the low WS290s all week.

children.title: Handymax
children.summary: Mediterranean Handymax vessels saw the TC6 index bottom out at WS218 from WS231 to then return to WS225 off the back of improved activity. Up on the Continent the TC23 index similarly had a big lump (circa 50 points) taken out if it early in the week WS270,s down to WS220s to then return back to WS231.25.

children.title: VLCC
children.summary: The VLCC market showed some optimism this week off the back of a thinner tonnage list, with the exception of the US Gulf to China route. 270,000mt Middle East Gulf to China market saw a 5.05 point improvement taking the index up to WS72.64, which shows an actual rise of about $6,200 per day with a TCE of $56,017 basis the Baltic Exchange's vessel description.

children.title: Suezmax
children.summary: The Suezmax market was somewhat fragmented this week and freight rates have slipped. The rate for 135,000mt CPC/Augusta eased an incremental 2.95 points to the WS157.61 mark (a round-trip TCE of about $78,434 per day).

children.title: Aframax
children.summary: In the North Sea market rates for the 80,000mt Hound Point/Wilhelmshaven route dropped about 3.75 points to the WS170 region (a round-trip daily TCE of $67,018). In the Mediterranean, the rate for 80,000mt Ceyhan/Lavera crumbled about 28 points to around the WS185 mark (a daily round trip TCE of $59,300).
